arts.ttl: Lampard out to prove critics wrong at Coventry after Chelsea ‘babysitting’
arts.sum: Appointed manager on two-and-a-half-year deal ‘British managers face more stringent opinion’ Frank Lampard said he believes former high-profile players face harsher criticism as managers, after expressing his excitement at relaunching his career at Coventry having been reduced to “babysitting” Chelsea in his last coaching role. Lampard, who took training on Thursday, has signed a two-and-a-half-year contract to succeed Mark Robins at the Championship club. The former Chelsea and England midfielder said he is au fait with the scrutiny that will accompany his return to work. The 46-year-old, whose first manager’s job was in the second tier with Derby, has been out of work since leaving his role as interim head coach at Chelsea at the end of 2022-23. “British managers should be given a chance and sometimes they face even more stringent opinion against them, especially ex-players,” he said. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Preview: Coventry City vs. Cardiff City - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's Championship clash between Coventry City and Cardiff City, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard sends message to Coventry doubters and explains how he'll prove them wrong
arts.sum: Frank Lampard joins Coventry against a backdrop of anger that the Sky Blues dispensed with the services of former boss Robins - and news of his arrival has been met with a largely lukewarm response

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard sets out his plans for Coventry City on back of foundations laid by Mark Robins
arts.sum: It’s a young squad but I always enjoy working with young players, which I did at Derby and Chelsea and I want them to be better. Frank Lampard has laid out his plans to take forward what he says is a “young and tactically adaptable” Coventry City squad, building on the foundations set down by his predecessor Mark Robins.

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard reveals his determination to 'prove people wrong' in his latest shot at coaching with Coventry as former Chelsea boss returns to the dugout
arts.sum: TOM COLLOMOSSE: The former Chelsea and England midfielder has signed a two-and-a-half-year deal with the Championship club , where he will have the title 'head coach'.
Coventry City appoint ex-Chelsea and England midfielder Frank Lampard as their head coach, following Mark Robins' sacking.

arts.ttl: Coventry City LIVE updates with Frank Lampard's first words as Doug King speaks out
arts.sum: Today's club press release read: " Coventry City are delighted to announce the appointment of Frank Lampard as the Club’s new Head Coach. "This followed an illustrious playing career, which saw him represent Chelsea 648 times and score 211 goals – becoming their record all-time goal-scorer.
